message: Can you believe it? That influencer photoshoot is blowing up! We're making waves all over social media.
Our watches and loafers are all over everyone's feeds
You're telling me? My inbox is packed with customer queries

Welcome to Discount Disaster, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

This is not due to sale

Thats definitely not due to sale
Did we look at all changes ? Teams were running load test
We are reviewing the recent changes and narrowing down the root cause

Service: FrontEndService

Environment: UAT

I was looking at traffic data - can some one give me data of traffic split across countries please
I have a paused all tests - i still see the errors
It is linked to a load test which was running, we are stopping that asap. Will be reviewed in the PM to see our best practices around UAT/PROD tier
But, i can say, our apps couldn't handle more than 4k requests- so definitely 6k is over loaded
is the 4k based on a config ?

can we check the routing config if there is an underlying routing issue ?

Definitely our front end cannot handle this much load
@Sree  the traffic is from Spain

or there is a config limiting the load
This looks suspicious to me
Can we get someone from security to review this @maya can you help to spot check the source from Spain

:star: Form an effective investigation team
Hey @s.sasi-bloomberg.net, after reviewing the logs, I strongly suspect that this is a security-related issue.
The abnormally high traffic with http 429, 503 and 504 errors may be due to a possible DDoS/DoS attack
I'll need to continue investigating further, but it definitely looks like something is targeting our system
Can we block that traffic ?
Looks like we have a security threat - We should be extra careful with this incident
Do we know what's the financial impact of the issue? How much are we losing every minute?
We dont have geo blockign enabled
@s.sasi-bloomberg.net Should we inform the 'Compliance Team' about this issue?
do we know the IP address?
The Compliance Team makes sure we follow laws and rules, like reporting the incident to the right people and keeping proper records. It helps us avoid legal trouble and protects our reputation.
Yes, we are under a DDoS attack sourcing from Spain. We should have compliance take a lot at it

From my logs earlier .
149.34.52.176

We have found the source IP and are blocking that

Looks like we have recovered
